figures.caption: Figure 4: Patchified vs. original matrices (Solar). Patchification (Eq. (24)) reshapes local patches into rows of a patch-matrix. In the patchified view (left), many rows/columns become visually near-repetitions due to recurring local textures, while the original matrices (right) remain globally diverse.

Figure 5: Consistent rank reduction via patchification. Normalized spectra (λi/λ1) show that the patchified representation decays faster: for i ≥ 2, the patchified curve lies consistently below the original, indicating that more energy concentrates in the leading components and the representation has a lower effective rank.

Figure 11: Comparison of generated samples across methods. Left: Solar Flare with pmiss = 40%. CoreFlow is trained at ρ = 9% of the original dimension, and is compared against MissDiff and MissFlow trained at ρ = 100%. Right: Case Waves with pmiss = 0% (complete training matrices). CoreFlow is trained at ρ = 1.44% of the original dimension, and is compared against CSDM trained at ρ ∈ {1.44%, 64%}.

Figure 10: Comparisons of generated samples on Solar Flare with pmiss = 0% (training on complete matrices). CoreFlow is trained at ρ = 9% of the original dimension, and is compared against CSDM trained at ρ ∈ {9%, 36%}.

Figure 2: True samples (left) and CoreFlow-generated samples (right) on real and synthetic benchmarks. The close visual agreement shows that CoreFlow captures the matrix distributions well even under aggressive dimensionality reduction and substantial training missingness.

Figure 3: Performance–efficiency tradeoff on Solar among generative models. The horizontal axis is the learneddimension ratio as a proxy for generative-model cost, and the vertical axis is test singular-value discrepancy (lower is better). Full training times are reported in Appendix C.4.

Table 5: Measured total training time on a single NVIDIA V100 GPU. For CoreFlow, the total includes both Stage I subspace training and Stage II core-space CNF training. For MissDiff, MissFlow, and CSDM, we report the actual generative model’s training time until validation-loss convergence.
Table 4: Neural Networks parameter counts across different datasets and rank settings. Notably, all models compared share the exact same network structure.
